name: Gewurzte Schweinsrippchen, Braised Spicy Spareribs
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Spare ribs, cracked in center
recipeInstructions:
1. Set out a heavy skillet and a roasting pan with a tight-fitting cover.
2. Cut the ribs into serving-size pieces. Coat the meat evenly with a mixture of flour, salt, and pepper.
3. Pour cooking oil in the heavy skillet. Add the ribs to the oil and brown slowly on both sides. While the meat is browning, prepare the meat broth by adding ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar, celery salt, cayenne pepper, cloves, allspice, bay leaf, minced garlic, and chopped onion to the broth mixture.
4. Put the meat into the roasting pan. Pour the broth mixture over the browned ribs. Add the chopped onion. Cover and put in a 350°F oven for about 1-1/2 hours, or until the ribs are tender.
5. With a slotted spoon, remove meat to a warm serving platter. Set aside to keep warm while preparing the sauce.
6. For Sauce: Skim excess fat from cooking liquid if necessary. Strain the liquid and pour into a small saucepan. Mix 1/4 cup water and 2 tablespoons flour in a screw-top jar. Cover jar tightly and shake until mixture is well blended.
7. Bring liquid in saucepan to boiling; stirring constantly, slowly pour half of the flour mixture into the cooking liquid. Bring to boiling. Gradually add the remaining flour mixture. Bring sauce to boiling after each addition. Cook 3 to 5 minutes.
8. Spoon or pour about half of the hot sauce over ribs on the platter. Serve remaining sauce in a gravy boat if desired. Serve ribs with Spätzle.
